The '''''Empire Forever''''' was an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] that served as [[Imperial Advisor/Legends|Imperial Advisor]] [[Coh Veshiv]]'s [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] under the [[Commander/Legends|command]] of [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Annance]]. Veshiv used the [[Corvette/Legends|corvette]] during his tour away from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] after the [[Advisor/Legends|Advisor]] was dispatched for irritating [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] himself. The ''Empire Forever'' was present at the planet [[Esseles/Legends|Esseles]] in [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]] for Veshiv's transfer to the Imperial [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate]] ''[[Far Orbit]]''; however, the ''Far Orbit'' was actually a [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[Privateer/Legends|privateer]] [[Starship/Legends|vessel]], and the crew of the [[Starship/Legends|ship]] [[Capture of Coh Veshiv|kidnapped]] the unsuspecting Veshiv.

==Description==
The ''Empire Forever'' was a [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] produced by the [[Corellian Engineering Corporation/Legends|Corellian Engineering Corporation]], and it measured 150 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] in length. Capable of reaching speeds up to 1,000 [[Kilometer per hour/Legends|kilometers per hour]] in a [[Planet/Legends|planetary]] [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]], the ''Empire Forever'' also boasted a [[Hyperdrive/Legends|Class 2 hyperdrive]], as well as [[Sensor/Legends|sensors]], a [[Deflector shield generator/Legends|deflector shield generator]], and a [[Navigation computer/Legends|navigation computer]]. The [[Corvette/Legends|corvette]] was modified for speed and durability, and was armed with six double [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] [[Cannon/Legends|cannons]], three at the front, one on each side, and one at the aft.

The [[Starship/Legends|ship]] had a cargo capacity of 3,000 [[Metric ton/Legends|metric tons]] and could carry supplies enough to last its crew of forty, including its twelve [[Gunner/Legends|gunners]], for eight [[Standard month/Legends|months]]. Possessing room for eighteen passengers and equipped with [[Life support/Legends|life support]], the ship usually brought along ten [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] guards, two [[Aide/Legends|aides]], and six [[TIE series/Legends|TIE Series]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[TIE pilot|pilots]] to crew the ''Empire Forever''{{'s}} complement of half a dozen [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighters]], which were housed in the corvette's [[Hangar/Legends|docking area]]; the hangar could also hold the [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] ''[[Myrenia]]''. The ''Empire Forever'' could operate with a skeleton crew of fifteen if needed. Additionally, the vessel had recreational areas, such as an [[arboretum]] featuring [[Plant/Legends|plant]] life from [[Imperial Advisor/Legends|Imperial Advisor]] [[Coh Veshiv]]'s [[Coh Veshiv's homeworld|homeworld]], as well as [[Force field/Legends|force fields]] and sensors that inhibited boarders.<ref name="Orbit" />

==History==
[[File:Empire_Forever_interior.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Coh Veshiv annoys Captain Annance aboard the ''Empire Forever''.]]
The ''Empire Forever'' served as an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] vessel<ref name="Orbit" /> around [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]],<ref name="Date">{{FarOrbitDate}}</ref> during the Galactic Empire's struggle against the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] in the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Imperial [[Advisor/Legends|Advisor]] Coh Veshiv traveled aboard the ''Empire Forever'', using the corvette as his [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], when he was sent away from the planet [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], ostensibly on a fact-finding mission; in reality, Veshiv had been dispatched because he had irritated [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] himself. Under the [[Commander/Legends|command]] of [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Annance]], the ''Empire Forever'' was ordered to remain out of combat as it ferried Veshiv about.<ref name="Orbit" />

Veshiv was not pleased that the ship he traveled aboard was a mere CR90 corvette. During the course of the ''Empire Forever''{{'s}} journey, Veshiv began to complain that he wanted at least a [[Cruiser/Legends|cruiser]] to be his ship of [[Transport/Legends|transport]], not a corvette. Three [[Standard month/Legends|months]] into their voyage—during which Veshiv had managed to thoroughly annoy the patient Annance—the corvette arrived at the planet [[Esseles/Legends|Esseles]].<ref name="Orbit" />

{{RPGseed}}
At Esseles, the ''Empire Forever'' was approached by the [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate]] ''[[Far Orbit]]'', an Imperial ship by design under the command of Captain [[Dhas Fenoep Vedij]]. Hailing the corvette, Vedij claimed that his ship had orders to take Veshiv, replacing the ''Empire Forever'' as the Advisor's transport. Glad to be rid of Veshiv and believing the orders were true, Annance sent Veshiv over to the ''Far Orbit'' in the Advisor's personal [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|''Lambda''-class T-4a shuttle]], ''Myrenia'', alongside his two aides, [[Vestig]] and [[Pich]], as well as Veshiv's stormtrooper guards. However, the ''Far Orbit'' was actually a [[Privateer/Legends|privateer]] vessel for the Alliance, and once the [[Frigate/Legends|frigate]] had jumped to [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]], they [[Capture of Coh Veshiv|captured]] Veshiv. The advisor's guards failed in their attempt to contact the ''Empire Forever'' for help as they were overwhelmed.<ref name="Orbit" />
{{Endgame}}

==Commanders and crew==
The ''Empire Forever'' was helmed by Captain Annance, a patient [[Gender/Legends|man]]. By the time the ''Empire Forever'' met the ''Far Orbit'' near Esseles, Annance was nonetheless quite annoyed by Coh Veshiv.<ref name="Orbit" />

==Behind the scenes==
The ''Empire Forever'' appeared in "[[The Capture of Coh Veshiv]]," an adventure written by [[Timothy S. O'Brien]] and published in the [[1998]] supplement to [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' ''[[The Far Orbit Project]]''. Although it is possible for the Imperials aboard the ''Far Orbit'' to contact the ''Empire Forever'' after the privateers try to capture them, this article assumes they failed in this matter as the book seems to indicate that they never got a communication out. Furthermore, although the scenario states that the ''Empire Forever''{{'s}} skeleton complement for gunners was 30, this number is clearly inaccurate, as it is greater than the amount for the gunners at full crew. Therefore, this article does not show the skeleton complement of gunners for the ''Empire Forever''.<ref name="Orbit" />
